headline: Alphabet’s New AI Chips And Agents Test Investor View On Valuation
summary: Google, part of Alphabet (NasdaqGS:GOOGL), introduced its new TPU 8t and TPU 8i AI chips at Cloud Next, alongside a unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. The company also launched a US$750 million fund aimed at speeding up enterprise adoption of AI agents. Google Cloud revealed new partnerships with Deloitte, Salesforce, Merck, Ulta Beauty, Oracle, and others to bring agentic AI tools into real world business operations. Alphabet, through Google Cloud, is pushing deeper into the AI...

headline: Top Dividend Stocks To Consider In April 2026
summary: The United States market has experienced a robust performance, rising 1.3% over the last week and an impressive 35% over the past year, with earnings anticipated to grow by 16% annually in the coming years. In such a dynamic environment, identifying strong dividend stocks involves looking for companies that not only offer attractive yields but also demonstrate stable financial health and consistent earnings growth.

headline: Merck to partner with Google Cloud on AI initiatives
summary: Drugmaker Merck & Co said on Wednesday it will partner with Google Cloud to build up its artificial intelligence capabilities, investing ‌as much as $1 billion with Google over a number of years to fund AI ‌infrastructure, engineers and licensing of the tech company's Gemini Enterprise platform.  The partnership, announced at Google's Cloud Next conference in ​Las Vegas, builds on work already underway between the companies and includes Google Cloud engineers working alongside Merck teams, executives from both companies said.

raw.headline: Merck to partner with Google Cloud on AI initiatives

The partnership, announced at Google's Cloud Next conference in ​Las Vegas, builds on work already underway between the companies and includes Google Cloud engineers working alongside Merck teams, executives from both companies said.

headline: Labcorp Launches FDA-Approved Companion Diagnostic to Identify Patients with Ovarian Cancer Eligible for KEYTRUDA®

headline: Why Merck (MRK) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
summary: Shares of global pharmaceutical company Merck (NYSE:MRK) fell 3.8% in the afternoon session after the company, along with its partner Eisai, announced that a late-stage clinical trial for a kidney cancer treatment failed to meet its primary goals.
